dcterms:title: Digatron CSV — BDF column mapping
dcterms:description: Maps Digatron battery cycler CSV export headers to BDF canonical columns. Digatron uses a '#' separator between column name and unit (e.g. 'Voltage#V'). Column headers appear on row 1; row 2 is a units row that the BDF parser drops. IMPORTANT: Digatron's AhAccu/WhAccu, AhBal/WhBal, and AhStep/WhStep do not align with the BDF cumulative/net/step definitions; the BDF normaliser recomputes all derived capacity and energy columns from the primary accumulators (AhCha, AhDch).

csvw:columns.csvw:name: test_time_second
csvw:columns.skos:note: The '#s'-suffixed column 'Program Duration#s' is emitted in milliseconds despite the 's' label (a ~1 s wall-clock advance increments the value by ~1000); it must be divided by 1000 for seconds. The schema:unitCode 's' therefore under-reports the scale for that variant and is pending the Digatron unit review.

csvw:columns.csvw:name: step_time_second
csvw:columns.skos:note: The '#s'-suffixed column 'Step Duration#s' is emitted in milliseconds despite the 's' label; divide by 1000 for seconds. Pending the Digatron unit review.

csvw:columns.csvw:name: unix_time_second
csvw:columns.skos:note: ISO 8601 datetime string; converted to Unix epoch seconds by the BDF normaliser.

csvw:columns.csvw:name: step_type
csvw:columns.skos:note: Status values indicate the step mode (e.g. charge, discharge, rest). The vocabulary is instrument-defined.

csvw:columns.csvw:name: step_cumulative_capacity_ah
csvw:columns.skos:note: Digatron AhStep is an unsigned within-step accumulator, matching the step_cumulative_capacity_ah definition (time integral of |I| from the step start). No recomputation is performed.

csvw:columns.csvw:name: net_capacity_ah
csvw:columns.skos:note: Digatron AhBal is an instrument-internal rolling balance that diverges from the BDF net definition; the BDF normaliser recomputes net_capacity_ah = AhCha − AhDch.

csvw:columns.csvw:name: cumulative_capacity_ah
csvw:columns.skos:note: Digatron AhAccu is the net signed running integral; the BDF normaliser recomputes cumulative_capacity_ah = AhCha + AhDch (throughput).

csvw:columns.csvw:name: step_cumulative_energy_wh
csvw:columns.skos:note: Digatron WhStep is an unsigned within-step accumulator, matching the step_cumulative_energy_wh definition (time integral of |P| from the step start). No recomputation is performed.
